# Improve coverage reports

## What this issue is about
This issue aims to enhance the coverage reports for the Plone project. Improved coverage reports help us understand how much of our code is tested, making it easier to maintain high-quality software.

## What needs to be done
The discussion suggests we should look into better tools or methods for generating and reporting code coverage. There is no specific implementation detail provided, so it's essential to explore options mentioned in the linked article: [Ditch Codecov for Python](https://hynek.me/articles/ditch-codecov-python/).

## Helpful background
Code coverage is a measure of how much of our code is tested by automated tests. The provided article offers insights into alternatives to existing coverage tools that may better fit our needs. The ongoing discussion lacks detailed suggestions, so further exploration and innovation are welcome.

## Good first steps
1. Read the linked article to understand potential improvements in coverage reporting.
2. Experiment with different coverage reporting tools or methods mentioned.
3. Join the discussion to share your findings or propose ideas.
4. Consider writing tests that help demonstrate improved coverage.

Feel free to reach out if you have questions or need guidance on where to start!
